programme.title: World Poker Tour
programme.desc: It’s another record-setting event on the World Poker Tour, as Season XV rolls on with the WPT Bay 101 Shooting Star. A field of 806 entries generated a prize pool of more than $5.7 million, and up for grabs is a first-place prize of nearly $1.4 million. WPT Champions Club member Sam Panzica headlines the final table in search of his second title this season, but he’ll have to go through a tough group of competitors that includes three-time WPT champion Chino Rheem.

programme.title: S3E1 Adventure Series
programme.desc: The GoPro production crew journeys to the Marum Crater on Ambrym Island in Vanuatu to join adventurer photographers Geoff Mackley and Bradley Ambrose, the first people to stand at the edge of a boiling lava lake and witness the raw lifeblood of our planet. The adventure continues with Dr. Ingrid Visser, who has dedicated her life and research to protecting the most intelligent predator in the ocean, the orca. She swims alongside wild orcas and champions modern solutions to free them from captivity. Experience never-before-seen footage captured by a GoPro mounted to the dorsal fin of a wild orca, along with rare archival material from the Orca Research Trust. This is exploration and conservation at its most powerful.

programme.title: S3E2 Adventure Series
programme.desc: The journey continues with Dr. Ingrid Visser, a marine pioneer devoted to protecting the ocean’s most intelligent predator, the orca. She swims with wild orcas and leads the charge for innovative solutions to end captivity. Witness never-before-seen footage captured by a GoPro mounted to the dorsal fin of a wild orca, paired with rare archival material from the Orca Research Trust. This is exploration and conservation at their most inspiring. In the wide-open skies and sweeping valleys of the Mustang Monument Wild Horse Eco-Resort & Sanctuary, more than 600 Wild American Mustangs roam free. Cowboy and family man Clay Nannini reveals the importance of preserving this symbol of American heritage while passing its legacy to his children. As night falls, Tokyo becomes an electrifying playground for those who crave speed and style. The city’s custom vehicle culture bursts to life, introducing unforgettable characters and rides unlike anything you have seen before. Lamborghinis, Hayabusas, RWB Porsches, and Kaido Racers create a visual feast on the streets of Tokyo after dark.

programme.title: Massacre Time 1966
programme.desc: In Massacre Time, Franco Nero blazes onto the Spaghetti Western scene as Tom Corbett, a drifter who returns home to find his family’s land stolen and his brother living in ruin. Together, the two face a ruthless tyrant and his sadistic son who rule with terror and bloodshed. Directed by Lucio Fulci, this violent, gripping tale delivers brutal gunfights, raw emotion, and the relentless pursuit of vengeance.

programme.title: The Desperate Mission 1969
programme.desc: In The Desperate Mission, Ricardo Montalbán stars as the legendary Joaquin Murrieta, the Mexican folk hero branded an outlaw by those who stole his land and destroyed his family. With a price on his head, he leads a daring fight against corruption, injustice, and betrayal across the California frontier. Filled with adventure, romance, and rebellion, this made-for-TV Western brings the legend of Murrieta to life with passion and fire.

With hard-riding action, blazing gunfights, and Maynard’s trademark grit, this classic delivers straight-shooting adventure on the lawless frontier.

programme.title: How Saran Wrap Saved D-Day
programme.desc: The search for an artificial form of quinine accidently creates the first artificial dye, Mauvine. A failed dry-cleaning fluid results in Saran Wrap. An attempt to grow bacteria in the lab leads to a cancer killing drug. And how nurses on the Western front invented sanitary towels.

programme.title: How Saran Wrap Saved D-Day
programme.desc: The search for an artificial form of quinine accidently creates the first artificial dye, Mauvine. A failed dry-cleaning fluid results in Saran Wrap. An attempt to grow bacteria in the lab leads to a cancer killing drug. And how nurses on the Western front invented sanitary towels.

programme.title: How Saran Wrap Saved D-Day
programme.desc: The search for an artificial form of quinine accidently creates the first artificial dye, Mauvine. A failed dry-cleaning fluid results in Saran Wrap. An attempt to grow bacteria in the lab leads to a cancer killing drug. And how nurses on the Western front invented sanitary towels.

programme.title: Christophe Rocancourt
programme.desc: Christophe Rocancourt is a French impostor and con artist who scammed rich and powerful people by masquerading in turn as a French nobleman, the heir to the Rockefeller family, or as a Russian prince.

programme.title: How Saran Wrap Saved D-Day
programme.desc: The search for an artificial form of quinine accidently creates the first artificial dye, Mauvine. A failed dry-cleaning fluid results in Saran Wrap. An attempt to grow bacteria in the lab leads to a cancer killing drug. And how nurses on the Western front invented sanitary towels.

programme.title: How Saran Wrap Saved D-Day
programme.desc: The search for an artificial form of quinine accidently creates the first artificial dye, Mauvine. A failed dry-cleaning fluid results in Saran Wrap. An attempt to grow bacteria in the lab leads to a cancer killing drug. And how nurses on the Western front invented sanitary towels.
